skill.name: web-search
skill.description: Use the inference.sh CLI to run Tavily and Exa web search, answer, and extraction apps for research workflows.
skill.summary: Search the web and extract page content through Tavily and Exa apps in the inference.sh CLI.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many shell and network patterns in SKILL.md. Most backtick findings are markdown code fences or inline command examples. The quick start includes a remote curl script piped to sh. The skill also depends on network-backed inference.sh apps. No evidence found for prompt injection, credential exfiltration, persistence, or malicious obfuscation.

medium_findings.title: Remote Installer Piped to Shell
medium_findings.description: The quick start tells users to pipe a remote installer into sh. This is a true positive because the script executes with user privileges. The install note mentions SHA-256 verification and manual install steps. This context suggests a risky installer pattern, not confirmed malicious intent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact curl pipe to sh pattern is present at line 16. Context indicates an installer flow, which is legitimate but still exposes users to remote code execution risk.

medium_findings.title: Network-Backed External CLI Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill runs infsh commands that call Tavily, Exa, OpenRouter, and inference.sh services. This is expected for web search. User queries and submitted URLs may still leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples clearly invoke external search, extraction, and LLM services. This is core functionality, but it creates privacy and data-handling risk for sensitive prompts or URLs.

low_findings.title: Static Markdown and Checksum False Positives
low_findings.description: Many Ruby backtick and weak crypto findings are markdown false positives. The referenced lines contain code fences, inline commands, tables, or SHA-256 wording. No Ruby execution or weak cryptographic algorithm use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are markdown examples and documentation, not executable Ruby source. The checksum reference is SHA-256, which is not a weak checksum algorithm in this context.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and Example URLs
low_findings.description: The skill contains hardcoded documentation links, an image asset, installer endpoints, and example.com sample URLs. These are visible documentation links. No evidence found for covert exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation and example inputs with no evidence of covert exfiltration. The network behavior is explicit and aligned with the skill purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: curl Piped Directly to sh
dangerous_patterns.description: The install command executes a remote script before user inspection. Prefer manual download, checksum verification, and explicit execution when possible.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present on line 16. The install note reduces concern about intent but does not remove the execution risk.

content.value_statement: Research tasks often need current web results and clean page content. This skill uses inference.sh apps for Tavily and Exa search, answers, and extraction inside Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Runs Tavily Search Assistant through the inference.sh CLI.
Runs Tavily Extract for clean text and image extraction from URLs.
Runs Exa Search for relevant web links with context.
Runs Exa Answer for direct factual answers.
Shows workflows that combine search or extraction results with LLM summarization.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and a logged-in account.
Sends search queries and extraction URLs to external services.
Search quality depends on Tavily, Exa, and the available web sources.
The quick start installer uses a remote script execution pattern.

use_cases.title: Source-Grounded Topic Research
use_cases.description: Collect recent links, summaries, and source context before writing a report or briefing.

use_cases.title: RAG Input Collection
use_cases.description: Find and extract page content that can be reviewed before adding it to a retrieval workflow.

use_cases.title: Claim Verification
use_cases.description: Search for supporting sources and compare extracted evidence against a specific claim.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web for recent information about [topic]. Return the main findings and cite the most useful sources.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Article Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the main content from these URLs: [URLs]. Summarize the key points and note any missing or inaccessible pages.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Sources
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for [question], compare the strongest sources, and separate confirmed facts from uncertain claims.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare RAG Research Inputs
prompt_templates.prompt: Find authoritative sources for [domain question], extract the relevant pages, and organize the results into themes for a RAG knowledge base.

output_examples.input: Search for current best practices for AI agent evaluation.
output_examples.output: A ranked source list, a short answer, and a summary of recurring evaluation methods across the sources.

output_examples.input: Extract content from two research article URLs.
output_examples.output: Clean article text summaries, important images when available, and notes about pages that could not be extracted.

output_examples.input: Verify whether a product announcement claim is accurate.
output_examples.output: A source-backed assessment that identifies supporting evidence, conflicting evidence, and unresolved points.

best_practices: Review search results before using them in reports or automated decisions.
Avoid sending secrets, private documents, or confidential URLs to external search services.
Use manual installer verification instead of piping remote scripts directly to a shell.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated answers as final without checking cited sources.
Do not extract private or access-controlled pages unless you have permission.
Do not use broad vague queries when a precise question or source type is available.

faq.question: Does this skill browse the live web?
faq.answer: It runs inference.sh apps that use Tavily and Exa services for web search and extraction.

faq.question: Does it require an account?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples require the inference.sh CLI and login before app commands can run.

faq.question: Can it extract full page content?
faq.answer: It can request extraction through Tavily or Exa, but results depend on page access and service support.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential data?
faq.answer: Use caution. Queries, URLs, and extracted content may be sent to external services.

faq.question: Why is the security rating medium?
faq.answer: The skill has expected network behavior, but its quick start pipes a remote installer script into sh.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
